skill.name: Azure Speech to Text REST API for Short Audio
skill.description: Use Azure Speech to Text REST API for transcribing short audio files up to 60 seconds. This skill provides Python code examples and integration patterns for speech recognition without the Speech SDK. Includes authentication, audio format requirements, error handling, and async support.
skill.summary: Transcribe short audio files using Azure Speech to Text REST API with Python
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill that provides guidance for integrating with Azure Speech to Text REST API. Static analysis found no suspicious patterns, no executable code, and no risk factors. The skill consists of documentation and code examples for legitimate API integration. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Transcribe Audio with Azure Speech to Text REST API
content.value_statement: Transcribe short audio files (up to 60 seconds) using Azure Speech to Text REST API without installing the Speech SDK. This skill provides ready-to-use Python code for authentication, audio formatting, and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Transcribe short audio files (up to 60 seconds) using Azure REST API
Handle authentication with subscription keys or bearer tokens
Support WAV PCM and OGG OPUS audio formats
Implement chunked transfer for lower latency
Process simple and detailed response formats
Handle errors and recognition status codes
limitations: No partial or interim results available
Does not support speech translation or custom models

use_cases.title: Transcribe Voice Memos
use_cases.description: Convert short voice recordings to text for note-taking and documentation
use_cases.target_user: Professionals who record voice notes and need quick transcription

use_cases.title: Automated Call Transcription
use_cases.description: Transcribe short phone call recordings for analysis and records
use_cases.target_user: Customer support teams and sales organizations

use_cases.title: Multilingual Content Transcription
use_cases.description: Transcribe audio content in multiple languages using Azure language support

prompt_templates.title: Basic Audio Transcription
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure Speech to Text REST API skill to transcribe the audio file at path [AUDIO_FILE_PATH] to text. Use language [LANGUAGE_CODE] (e.g., en-US).
prompt_templates.scenario: Transcribe a short audio file with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Transcription with Confidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure Speech to Text REST API skill to transcribe [AUDIO_FILE_PATH] using detailed format to get confidence scores. Language: [LANGUAGE]. Handle errors gracefully.
prompt_templates.scenario: Get detailed results including confidence scores for each transcription

prompt_templates.title: Async Transcription for Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure Speech to Text REST API skill to transcribe [AUDIO_FILE_PATH] asynchronously. Show how to implement the async version with aiohttp for better performance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implement async transcription for lower latency

prompt_templates.title: Custom Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure Speech to Text REST API skill to write a transcription function that handles all RecognitionStatus values (Success, NoMatch, InitialSilenceTimeout, BabbleTimeout, Error) with appropriate responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implement comprehensive error handling for production use

output_examples.input: Transcribe audio.wav using Azure Speech to Text with detailed format
output_examples.output:
Transcription successful!

Display Text: What's the weather like today?
Confidence: 90.5%

The audio has been transcribed using Azure Speech to Text REST API with detailed response format.

output_examples.input: How do I authenticate with Azure Speech to Text using bearer tokens?
output_examples.output:
You can authenticate using bearer tokens for improved security. The token is valid for 10 minutes. Here's how to implement it:

1. Get access token from the token endpoint
2. Use the token in the Authorization header
3. Cache the token and renew before expiration

This approach is recommended for production applications.

best_practices: Use WAV PCM format at 16kHz mono for best recognition accuracy
Cache bearer tokens for 9 minutes to avoid repeated authentication
Enable chunked transfer encoding for lower latency on larger files
anti_patterns: Do not send audio files longer than 60 seconds - use Batch Transcription API instead
Do not use this for real-time streaming - use Speech SDK streaming
Do not hardcode API keys in source code - use environment variables

faq.question: What audio formats does Azure Speech to Text REST API support?
faq.answer: Azure Speech to Text REST API supports WAV with PCM codec at 16kHz mono (recommended) and OGG OPUS codec. The audio must be no longer than 60 seconds.

faq.question: Do I need to install the Azure Speech SDK to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses the REST API directly with the requests library. No SDK installation is required. Simply install the requests package.

faq.question: How do I get Azure Speech to Text credentials?
faq.answer: Create an Azure subscription, then create a Speech resource in the Azure Portal. Go to the resource Keys and Endpoint page to get your API key and region.

faq.question: What is the difference between simple and detailed response format?
faq.answer: Simple format returns just the DisplayText. Detailed format returns confidence scores, lexical form, ITN (inverse text normalization), and masked ITN for each result.

faq.question: Can I transcribe audio in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure Speech to Text supports many languages. Specify the language using the language query parameter (e.g., de-DE for German, fr-FR for French).

faq.question: How do I handle authentication errors?
faq.answer: Check that your API key is correct and has not expired. Ensure the region in your URL matches your resource region. Use bearer tokens for production to avoid key exposure.
